skill.name: setup
skill.description: Guides project setup by classifying new or existing projects, scaffolding starter files, and generating workflow and Claude Code settings.
skill.summary: Initialize project structure, workflow files, and Claude Code settings for new or existing projects.
skill.author: Chachamaru127

security_audit.summary: Static analysis produced many false positives from Markdown backticks, YAML text, and documentation examples. However, the skill repeatedly recommends bypassPermissions, broad MCP wildcard allowances, and Claude Code settings changes that weaken permission boundaries. No prompt injection or confirmed malicious exfiltration was found, but publication should wait for maintainer review and safer defaults.

high_findings.title: Permission Bypass Recommended for Claude Code Settings
high_findings.description: The settings generator recommends bypassPermissions, avoids putting Edit and Write under ask, and suggests claude --dangerously-skip-permissions. This weakens normal confirmation boundaries for a skill that also writes project settings.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly instructs users to enable bypassPermissions and dangerously skip permissions. The intent appears operational rather than malicious, but the security impact is direct.

high_findings.title: Broad MCP Wildcard Tool Allowances
high_findings.description: The skill documents wildcard allows for entire MCP servers, including Supabase tools that can query or apply migrations. Broad allow patterns increase impact if a connected server exposes sensitive operations.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The wildcard allow examples are explicit and include high-impact MCP servers. The risk depends on installed MCP servers, so confidence is high but not absolute.

medium_findings.title: Local Shell Commands in Setup Flow
medium_findings.description: The documentation instructs running a project analysis script, Claude plugin installs, jq or python validation, and copy commands. These commands are mostly legitimate setup actions but require user review before execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible setup instructions rather than hidden execution. Risk remains because the skill is allowed Bash and references a plugin script path.

medium_findings.title: Project Files and Hidden Settings Are Written
medium_findings.description: The skill creates workflow files, starter project files, and .claude settings files. This is core functionality, but changes can affect repository behavior and AI tool permissions.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The write targets are clearly documented and aligned with the skill purpose. The risk is moderate because generated settings can alter permission behavior.

low_findings.title: Many Static Alerts Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: Most Ruby backtick, weak cryptography, entropy, and path traversal alerts map to Markdown fences, inline code, Japanese prose, or documentation paths. I did not find encoded payloads or real weak cryptographic operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows these locations are documentation examples and front matter, not executable code. The scanner appears to treat Markdown syntax as code patterns.

low_findings.title: External Documentation URL Only
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL points to Claude Code documentation and is not used for data transfer. No evidence found of network exfiltration in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is presented as a documentation reference. There is no fetch, curl, webhook, or instruction to send project data to that URL.

dangerous_patterns.title: Permission Bypass Mode Guidance
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill promotes bypassPermissions and the dangerously-skip-permissions flag as part of setup guidance. This is dangerous when bundled with automated settings generation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The risky permission mode is explicitly recommended in multiple locations. The behavior is not inferred from ambiguous syntax.

dangerous_patterns.title: Wildcard MCP Allow Patterns
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill shows full-server MCP wildcard permissions instead of narrow tool permissions. This can grant broad access to database or browser automation tools.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The wildcard patterns are concrete examples. Actual impact depends on which MCP servers are installed by the user.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hidden Claude Settings Modification
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill creates or updates .claude/settings.json and recommends a local settings file for bypassPermissions. Hidden tool settings can change future AI agent behavior in the repository.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem targets and intended setting changes are explicitly documented. The concern is configuration risk, not concealed behavior.

content.value_statement: New projects need consistent workflow docs, settings, and starter structure before work begins. This skill guides setup, scaffolding, and Claude Code configuration with project-aware checks.
actual_capabilities: Classifies a project as new, existing, or ambiguous before setup.
Asks the user to choose project type when detection is unclear.
Generates AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, and Plans.md workflow files.
Creates starter project structure and common configuration files.
Creates or merges .claude/settings.json with permission policy guidance.
Preserves existing settings and asks before overwriting workflow files.
limitations: Some guidance recommends relaxed Claude Code permission modes.
It depends on templates and scripts outside the reviewed Markdown files.
It does not install package dependencies without user confirmation.
It is mainly documented in Japanese, which may require translation for some teams.

use_cases.title: Start a New Application
use_cases.description: Create starter folders, baseline files, and workflow documents before implementation begins.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Agent Workflows
use_cases.description: Generate shared AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, and Plans.md files for consistent AI-assisted development.

use_cases.title: Prepare Claude Code Permissions
use_cases.description: Create or merge Claude Code settings while keeping existing hooks, environment settings, and model fields.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Project Setup
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new repository.
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up this new project and generate the standard workflow files.

prompt_templates.title: Existing Project Setup
prompt_templates.scenario: Add workflow files without overwriting current work.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this existing project, then add missing workflow files without replacing current files.

prompt_templates.title: Claude Code Settings Merge
prompt_templates.scenario: Update Claude Code settings while preserving existing configuration.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create or update .claude/settings.json, preserve existing settings, and explain every permission change before applying it.

prompt_templates.title: Full Adaptive Setup Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced setup with explicit security review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run adaptive setup for this repository, show the proposed file changes, flag permission risks, and wait for approval before writing files.

output_examples.input: Set up a new TypeScript web app workflow.
output_examples.output: A setup summary with detected stack, created workflow files, starter folders, and next steps for dependency installation.

output_examples.input: Add Claude Code settings to an existing repository.
output_examples.output: A merge report that lists preserved settings, added permission rules, backup handling, and validation results.
A short question asking whether to treat the repository as new or existing, with the detected reason shown.

best_practices: Review proposed file changes before allowing writes.
Use narrow permission rules instead of broad wildcard allows.
Keep generated local settings out of version control when they change permissions.
anti_patterns: Do not enable bypass permissions without a project-specific security review.
Do not allow entire MCP servers when only one tool is needed.
Do not run generated install commands before checking the selected stack.

faq.question: What does this skill generate?
faq.answer: It can generate workflow files, starter project structure, and Claude Code settings for a repository.

faq.question: Does it overwrite existing files?
faq.answer: The documentation says existing workflow files should require confirmation before replacement.

faq.question: Why is the security risk high?
faq.answer: It recommends bypass permission modes and broad MCP wildcard allows, which can weaken safety boundaries.

faq.question: Does it send data to a network service?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The reviewed URL is a documentation reference, not an exfiltration endpoint.

faq.question: Can it be used with Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The metadata lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: What should teams review first?
faq.answer: Review .claude settings changes, MCP wildcard permissions, and any Bash commands before running setup.
